title: 7 ex-AAP MPs formally join BJP; NDA strength in RS rises to 148, Kejriwal's party down to 3
content: Seven Aam Aadmi Party Rajya Sabha MPs have officially joined the Bharatiya Janata Party, bolstering the NDA's numbers in the Upper House. This move, facilitated by the anti-defection law's provision for two-thirds party mergers, sees the BJP's strength increase significantly. The party anticipates further gains by year-end, nearing a crucial two-thirds majority.

title: Mamata's counter to PM Modi’s 'jhalmuri' break: A visit to local market ahead of Phase 2 polls
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee hit the streets of Kolkata, visiting a local market to connect with vendors and shoppers. She discussed prices of essentials and assured continued state support. This move comes amid a political battle over cultural identity, with Banerjee also criticizing Prime Minister Modi's recent outreach events.

title: One trick at a time, Krunal Pandya shows why skill, not match-ups, matters
content: Krunal Pandya has stood out for Royal Challengers Bengaluru in IPL 2026 by adapting brilliantly in a batter-dominated game. With clever variations, bouncers, and sharp tactics, he has handled all match situations. Despite higher economy, his fearless approach and innovation have impressed, with Dinesh Karthik calling him a “trend-setter” redefining finger-spin. Krunal now aims for an India comeback.

title: OpenAI CEO is literally losing sleep over tech that he says will ‘collapse’ the economy
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman sparked debate with predictions of economic collapse post-AGI due to mass unemployment.  Ironically, he's adopting polyphasic sleep, claiming GPT-5.5's advancements are too compelling to miss.  This comes as OpenAI launched GPT-5.5, boasting enhanced reasoning and coding, outperforming rivals.

title: Comfort push: Shatabdi, Jan Shatabdi trains set for nationwide revamp
content: In a communication to all zones, the Railway Board has directed them to conduct detailed inspections of train rakes and prepare time-bound action plans to address deficiencies in passenger amenities.

title: Petrol, diesel prices today: Rates steady despite global oil surge - check city-wise list
content: Fuel prices held steady across major Indian cities on Monday, defying volatile global crude oil markets driven by Middle East tensions. Despite Brent crude exceeding $106 per barrel, domestic petrol and diesel rates remained unchanged. This stability, however, is precarious, with potential hikes looming if international oil prices continue their ascent.

title: 'Hope of getting justice shattered': Kejriwal refuses to appear before court, writes to HC judge
content: Former Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal has refused to appear before the Delhi High Court in the excise policy case, citing a loss of hope for justice. He stated his decision to follow Mahatma Gandhi's path of Satyagraha and will appeal the order in the Supreme Court.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ crosses Rs 1130 cr mark amid ‘Michael,’ ‘Bhooth Bangla’ buzz
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its phenomenal run, crossing Rs 1130 crore in India and nearing Rs 1780 crore globally after 39 days. The film demonstrated remarkable growth over its sixth weekend, outperforming competition. Despite new releases, its enduring popularity is evident in its sustained box office success.

title: 'Bhaiya nahi, brother’: Rinku's family call with Narine steals show - Watch
content: Rinku Singh starred in Kolkata Knight Riders’s thrilling win over Lucknow Super Giants, scoring an unbeaten 83 and taking four catches. Sunil Narine sealed the Super Over by conceding just one run. After the match, a viral video showed Rinku correcting his sister from “bhaiya” to “brother” while praising Narine, leaving everyone laughing.

title: Zoho founder’s open letter to Indians in America on future opportunities
content: Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu has urged Indians in the US to return home, citing changing immigration perceptions and the need for their expertise to boost India's technological progress. He emphasized that global respect for Indians hinges on India's own development, calling for professionals to contribute their skills to the nation's future prosperity and civilisational strength.

title: Raghav Chadha addresses backlash on joining BJP in new video, cites ‘toxic work environment’ in AAP
content: Raghav Chadha explained his departure from the Aam Aadmi Party, citing a toxic work environment and corruption within the organization. He stated that he felt like the 'right man, but in the wrong party,' and that seven MPs have decided to leave. Chadha asserted the decision was driven by disappointment, not fear, and pledged to continue public service.

title: MBA student raped after grocery delivery; 67-year-old shopkeeper arrested
content: A 67-year-old grocery shop owner faces charges of rape after allegedly assaulting an MBA student in her Bhubaneswar rented room. The incident occurred when he delivered grocery items. The student, who was alone, was attacked after she went to fetch water. The accused was arrested and remanded in judicial custody.

title: IPL security scare: 240 CCTV cameras sabotaged during RCB vs GT match
content: Over 240 CCTV cameras at M Chinnaswamy Stadium were rendered non-functional on April 24. This disruption affected security surveillance during the IPL match between Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Gujarat Titans. Two individuals working for a sub-vendor were apprehended. They allegedly damaged Network Video Recorder systems and fibre connections. The accused claimed non-payment of dues as the reason for their actions.

title: Price prediction: Will gold prices continue to be volatile? Check outlook for this week
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices will closely track movements on the rate decisions by several central banks, including the US Federal Reserve, this week, says Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.

title: Pant told to give up LSG captaincy, Ex-India cricketer names replacement
content: Rishabh Pant’s struggles as Lucknow Super Giants captain continued after a fifth straight loss, with poor home results and 14 defeats in 22 games. Former Indian player Wasim Jaffer suggested appointing Aiden Markram as captain, saying leadership pressure is affecting Pant’s batting and that he should focus solely on playing.

title: Jobless man kills dietician wife, seals body in cement box; letter sent to son reveals chilling plot
content: A dietician was murdered by her husband, who then concealed her body in a cement-filled wooden box at their old residence. The accused, Vishal Salvi, confessed to the crime in a letter sent by his son, citing domestic discord and his prolonged unemployment as potential motives. Police discovered the decomposed body after the confession.

title: 'Killers' of the womb: A son obsession
content: Deep-rooted son preference fuels sex-selective abortions in India, leading to millions of fewer female births by 2030. Despite legal bans, illegal networks facilitate prenatal sex determination and terminations, driven by cultural pressures and financial anxieties. While some areas show slow improvement in sex ratios, demand for sons persists.

title: 'Missing father-in-law a lot': Fiancée Priya after Rinku’s heroics - Watch
content: Rinku Singh starred in Kolkata Knight Riders’ Super Over win over Lucknow Super Giants, scoring an unbeaten 83 and excelling in the field. The performance was emotional, dedicated to his late father, as shared by fiancée Priya Saroj. He smashed four sixes late, helped force a tie, contributed in the Super Over, and hit the winning boundary, earning Player of the Match honors.

title: Asian stocks today: Japan’s Nikkei surges to record high as markets stay mixed
content: Asian markets showed a mixed performance today. Japan's Nikkei 225 reached new record highs, boosted by technology stocks amid the AI boom. However, rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions, particularly concerning Iran, tempered investor sentiment across other regions. Investors are now keenly awaiting key central bank decisions this week.

title: American woman with four kids lists 10 lessons about living in India
content: In a charmingly candid Instagram post, Kristen Fischer, an American mother, shares the delightful lessons she's learned while living in India. Her viral insights redefine common terms like 'spicy,' 'five minutes,' and personal space, weaving an entertaining tapestry of cultural revelations.

title: 'Criminal decision': Rishabh Pant faces heat for Nicholas Pooran call
content: Lucknow Super Giants suffered a heart-wrenching Super Over loss to Kolkata Knight Riders, with Nicholas Pooran's first-ball dismissal drawing sharp criticism from Dale Steyn. Steyn labelled the decision to send Pooran in as 'criminal' given his poor form, highlighting the missed opportunity for LSG to secure a crucial win and their subsequent slide to the bottom of the table.

title: 'Not a suitable mediator': Iran accuses Pakistan of bias towards US, says it 'went back on its word'
content: An Iranian MP has questioned Pakistan's impartiality as a mediator in US-Iran talks, citing Islamabad's perceived alignment with American interests. This comes as Iran's Foreign Minister visited Pakistan and Oman to advance diplomatic efforts, though US President Trump indicated Iran could initiate contact for negotiations.

title: Sun Pharma to acquire US-based Organon in $11.75bn deal
content: Indian pharmaceutical giant Sun Pharma is set to acquire US-based Organon & Co. for $11.75 billion in an all-cash deal. This landmark acquisition, one of India's largest overseas ventures, will propel Sun Pharma into the top 25 global pharma companies. The move significantly bolsters its presence in women's health and biosimilars, expanding its global reach across 150 countries.

title: ‘Our math is different’: Iran warns Gulf countries of ‘fourfold’ retaliation after Trump threat
content: Iran's Vice President warned of a fourfold retaliation against nations supporting damage to its infrastructure, particularly its oil sector, following US President Trump's remarks. This threat underscores heightened tensions around Iran's energy assets amidst ongoing conflict and diplomatic efforts, including a proposed two-stage plan to the US.

title: Before Kargil: How India handed Musharraf his first defeat in Siachen— a first-hand account
content: India secured the strategic Saltoro Ridge in 1984, leading to a fierce battle for Quaid Post. Operation Rajiv saw Indian soldiers, led by Naib Subedar Bana Singh, bravely capture the post in 1987 after intense fighting. Renamed Bana Post, its capture marked a significant Indian victory, thwarting Pakistani attempts to regain dominance.

title: 0, 0, 0, 1, 0: Pooran’s Super Over record raises questions over LSG call
content: Nicholas Pooran’s poor Super Over record, just one run in 10 balls, came under scrutiny after Lucknow Super Giants lost to Kolkata Knight Riders. Despite this, Rishabh Pant backed him, stressing team trust and accountability. Sunil Narine’s strikes restricted LSG to one run, and Rinku Singh sealed the win instantly.

title: Ronaldo’s 3 final career goals before retirement – Messi already ahead
content: Cristiano Ronaldo is entering the final phase of his career with three clear goals still driving him forward: a first World Cup title in 2026, the pursuit of 1,000 career goals, and the rare chance of playing alongside his son at Al Nassr. Even at 41, he remains central for club and country, shaping both legacy and milestones.

title: Dianna Russini remark on husband resurfaces amid Mike Vrabel buzz
content: Dianna Russini is facing renewed scrutiny as an old joke about marrying Kevin Goldschmidt for his “big wallet” resurfaces amid controversy involving Mike Vrabel. The remark, later clarified as humor, has been widely misinterpreted online. At the same time, rumors around leaked photos and her alleged link to Vrabel continue to circulate, though unverified, keeping the debate alive across social media and sports circles.

title: LeBron, Bronny make NBA history with first father-son playoff alley-oop
content: LeBron James and Bronny James made NBA playoff history with the league’s first father-son scoring connection, as LeBron set up an alley-oop finished by Bronny in Game 3 against the Houston Rockets. Beyond the highlight, the moment underscored LeBron’s unmatched longevity, allowing their careers to overlap in a meaningful, competitive playoff setting rather than a symbolic appearance.

title: America's oldest man reveals 3 habits that helped him live long
content: At 111 years old, Luis Cano stands as America's oldest man, shedding light on his incredible lifespan through three straightforward habits: steering clear of smoking and drinking, valuing restful sleep, and cultivating positive emotions and relationships. His wise approach emphasizes that it's not the latest health fads but these core practices that sustain a long and meaningful life.

title: What is Iran’s latest proposal to US to end war? Ceasefire first, nuclear talks later
content: Iran has reportedly offered the US a new proposal focusing on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ending the war, postponing nuclear negotiations. This move aims to bypass internal divisions within Iran regarding nuclear concessions, though it could diminish US leverage in future talks.

title: 'Purchased' for Rs 1 lakh, toddler left on highway by bizman, wife for 'bringing bad luck'
content: A Guna couple abandoned their toddler on a highway, believing she brought bad luck. Investigations revealed the child was allegedly purchased for Rs 1 lakh through an illegal network, expanding the case into a suspected child trafficking racket. The girl was found alone and is now in the care of the Child Welfare Committee.

title: Tollywood actor booked for duping NRI techie of Rs 9.35cr with marriage promise
content: A Tollywood actress and Bigg Boss contestant has been booked for allegedly defrauding a London-based software professional of Rs 9.35 crore. The victim claims the actress promised marriage and sought financial assistance for her career and personal expenses. Despite multiple attempts to finalize marriage and recover funds, the actress allegedly evaded the complainant and failed to honor repayment commitments.

title: Oil prices today: Crude jumps nearly 2% as US-Iran talks stall
content: Global oil prices surged Monday, with Brent crude hitting $107.49 and WTI reaching $96.17, driven by stalled US-Iran peace talks and ongoing Strait of Hormuz disruptions.  This supply squeeze, exacerbated by restricted shipping, has led Goldman Sachs to revise its Q4 oil price forecasts upwards, signaling continued market volatility.

title: Raghuvanshi out explained: Should Pant have withdrawn the appeal?
content: With Raghuvanshi given out after a mid-dive deflection, the decision raised questions on law versus spirit and whether Rishabh Pant should have withdrawn the appeal. Rohan Gavaskar disagreed with the call, while Virender Sehwag termed it harsh. KKR later won in a Super Over.

title: West Bengal polls: ‘Give me votes, I’ll give you freedom,’ says PM Modi in Matua belt
content: In a powerful rally in Bengal, Prime Minister Narendra Modi echoed Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose's rallying cry, passionately urging voters in the Matua region to cast their ballots for 'freedom' from what he described as the TMC's oppressive 'jungle raj.

title: 'Nandri Chennai': Gill shares special post with yellow whistle
content: Shubman Gill celebrated Gujarat Titans’ win over Chennai Super Kings with a “Nandri Chennai” post. GT chased 159 comfortably, winning by eight wickets with 20 balls to spare. Sai Sudharsan starred with 87, supported by Gill and Jos Buttler. Earlier, Ruturaj Gaikwad made 74*, but Kagiso Rabada’s strikes restricted CSK.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for April 27 week - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: For the trading week starting April 27, 2026, Tata Steel, and Cyient DLM are the top stock picks from Motilal Oswal Wealth Management Research Desk.

title: Hyderabad comic’s show in Bengaluru disrupted over 2-year-old joke on Andhra Pradesh CM
content: Stand-up comedian Sarat Uday's show in Bengaluru was disrupted by alleged TDP protesters over past jokes about Andhra Pradesh CM N Chandrababu Naidu. Despite an earlier apology, Uday was forced to apologize again on stage. The party president condemned the protest but cautioned against comments on sensitive issues.

title: Raghu Rai's pictures made sure truth of Bhopal gas tragedy couldn't be buried: NGOs
content: “Raghu Rai was a steadfast ally of the Bhopal campaign. He captured the immediate and unbearable human cost of the December 1984 Union Carbide disaster and returned, time and again, to reveal the long arc of suffering, official neglect and the determined resistance of survivors seeking accountability”

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: I will come again during oath-taking ceremony of BJP government, says PM Modi at Barrackpore rally
content: “The Election Commission of India has sought a report on the incident, while the police said it is yet to be ascertained who fired the shot,” an official said

title: AAP no longer the same old party; has a toxic work environment, says Raghav Chadha
content: “But today, this party is no longer the same old party. Today, there’s a toxic work environment in this party. You are stopped from working. You are stopped from speaking in Parliament,” Raghav Chadha said

title: TMC lodges complaint against BJP candidate over alleged misuse of national flag
content: TMC’s Kohinoor Mazumdar alleges BJP’s Papia Adhikari was seen wiping her face with a dupatta bearing the Indian tricolour, terms it a violation of the Prevention of Insults to National Honour Act

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Over 50 lakh more people voted this time compared to 2024 Lok Sabha polls
content: In the State, people’s interest in polling process has always been higher for Assembly elections when compared to Lok Sabha polls, show data from elections held over the past 20 years; parties carry out a more intensive mobilisation for State polls

title: Supreme Court takes suo motu note of assault on woman advocate, issues directions
content: The Supreme Court directed the Delhi Police Commissioner to hand over the probe to a senior police person, preferably a woman officer in the rank of ACP or DCP

title: 66% turnout for civic polls as people brave heat to vote in Gujarat
content: Polling in municipal corporations comparatively lower than in other local bodies: SEC; AAP candidate among three reported dead on polling day

title: Trinamool running a reign of terror in West Bengal, says  Rahul Gandhi
content: Condoling the death of a Congress worker in Asansol who the party has claimed was beaten up by ‘miscreants’ linked to the ruling Trinamool, the Leader of the Opposition in Lok Sabha said his party will not bow  before ‘this politics that stains India’s non-violent tradition’

title: Kerala’s SARPA app to link doctors, experts for real-time snakebite guidance
content: The Snake Awareness, Rescue and Protection App has played a crucial role in recent years by coordinating snake rescues, enabling users to report sightings, and providing details of trained responders and nearby healthcare facilities

title: Palacode, Veerapandi and Kulithalai frontrunners in voter turnout since 2011
content: They saw high turnout in every Assembly poll held in the State since the 2008 delimitation. This is due to several factors, including political mobilisation on communal lines and local-level loyalties

title: Partition in 1947 result of ‘population imbalance’: RSS leader Sunil Ambekar
content: “Illegal infiltration and religious conversions carried out through “force, inducement and deception” are also matters of concern, as these factors also create an imbalance in the population,” he said

title: Mumbai court orders defreezing of Rhea Chakraborty's bank account
content: The Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) had frozen the duo's accounts as part of its probe into a drug-related case linked to the death of actor Sushant Singh Rajput

title: Counting underway for Aizawl Municipal Corporation polls, ZPM leads in 5 wards
content: A total of 62.77% of the 2.39 lakh voters exercised their franchise in the AMC polls held on April 21

title: Panic buying shuts 421 petrol bunks in Andhra Pradesh; CM Naidu orders review
content: Panic buying has pushed Andhra Pradesh’s petrol and diesel sales 50% above the daily average, shutting 421 of the State’s 4,510 outlets

title: Fire breaks out near parking area at Thiruvananthapuram railway station in Kerala
content: Blaze quickly brought under control, say officials. The fire reportedly originated from a heap of waste near the parking area

title: Three killed as motorbike hits pedestrian on Mumbai’s Marine Drive
content: The accident occurred at 5.40 A.M. at the Parsi Gymkhana junction signal on N S Road, when the pedestrian was crossing the road, an official said.

title: Won’t appear in person or through lawyer: Kejriwal writes to Delhi HC Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma
content: The AAP convenor and former Delhi Chief Minister said he has made the decision by listening to the voice of his conscience

title: Will AAP MPs face disqualification after joining BJP?
content: Is there a law against defection? What are the exceptions to it and how has the Supreme Court interpreted them? What are experts’ view on seven of AAP’s 10 Rajya Sabha members joining the BJP last week?

title: ED raids in case against former Punjab Police DIG Bhullar, linked entities
content: 11 premises in Chandigarh, Ludhiana, Patiala, Nabha and Jalandhar linked to the accused, his associates are being covered under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA)

title: Man alleges Telugu actor of defrauding ₹9.35 crore from his son on pretext of marriage
content: According to the complaint, Y.V. Dharmendra, a software engineer working in London, met Ashu Reddy in 2018. Within two months, she allegedly proposed marriage and continued the relationship despite being informed about his earlier marriage and ongoing divorce.

title: Raghu Rai didn’t just photograph India, he invented a way of seeing it
content: The audacity of Raghu Rai’s wide-angle lens, the tension he seemed to choreograph at the edges of the frame added a cinematic aside long before cinema caught up with that language.

title: Youth Congress stages ‘sleep protest’ at KSEB office over alleged unannounced power cuts in Kerala’s Kollam
content: Protesters alleged that ‘the only place where the electricity supply remains uninterrupted’ is KSEB offices and, in light of this, decided to sleep there to avoid power cuts during the night

title: Suspected West Nile fever cases trigger health warning
content: The cases were reported from Elanji, Eroor in Thripunithura, Pallikkara in Kizhakkambalam, Ponekkara in Edappally, and Palluruthy

title: Disqualify all seven defecting MPs, AAP says in petition to Rajya Sabha Chairman
content: Group led by Raghav Chadha claims that it fulfills required two-third strength making the merger with the BJP legal; however, the AAP has said that such a decision has to be taken by the party

title: Ghazipur teen death: Prohibitory order issued, protests banned; visit to victim's village barred
content: The government issued a statement, saying "unverified rumours" are being spread by certain "mischievous elements" and Opposition parties are "attempting to sensationalise the matter"

title: Azim Premji University to host Sahitya Sahavasa in Mysuru on Apr 28
content: The day-long programme to be inaugurated by former Vice Chancellor of Sanskrit University Dr. Padma Shekar will celebrate the literary contributions of eminent Kannada writers

title: ₹5 lakh compensation for families of two youths who were struck by lightning during rain in north Karnataka
content: The two youths were struck by lightning while taking shelter under a mango tree in a farmland amid sudden rainfall

title: BITS Pilani opens smart manufacturing centre in Bengaluru
content: The centre would offer short 2-3-day intensive training programmes and six-month-long courses, in alignment with the needs of the industry

title: UAE-Iran differences and India’s plans to dilute language on Israel-Palestine derail joint statement at BRICS meet
content: Deeply polarised deputy FMs meeting in Delhi spell trouble for India’s BRICS Presidency; as the language of a joint statement eluded diplomats, India issued a ‘Chair’s Statement’ that reflected the subjects discussed

title: Andhra University centenary: Vice-President, Sachin Tendulkar at April 27 event
content: Vice-President and Sachin Tendulkar to attend valedictory at engineering college grounds, nearly 25,000 expected; campus illuminated, MoUs and commemorative releases planned

title: Overfed and undernourished in Delhi
content: India’s capital faces what public health professionals call the Double Burden of Malnutrition, a condition in which children over-consume calories that lack essential nutrients. Combined with the lack of physical activity, the addictive nature of junk food, and parents unaware of nutritional realities, this early undernutrition is a risk factor for obesity later in life. A report on Delhi’s obesity-without-nutrition problem that needs immediate attention

title: Election Commission announces tight security in 142 seats ahead of second phase of Bengal polls
content: During a high-level coordination meeting, attended by poll officials, senior police officers and commanders of central forces from three election districts under Kolkata, the ECI reviewed the preparedness in 142 constituencies that would go to polls on April 29

title: Census 2027: self-enumeration window open till April 30, public urged to use online portal
content: NTR District Joint Collector S. Ilakkiya flagged off a 5-km run in Vijayawada to spread awareness about the Census 2027 self-enumeration form, which can be filled online until April 30

title: Rahul Gandhi plans Great Nicobar visit, clearance awaited
content: The Union government is planning a ₹92,000-crore mega infrastructure project in Great Nicobar Island amid tribal communities’ concerns that they will be displaced and lose their rights over the forests there

title: Rajasthan may serve as “living lab” for farm tech companies: CM Bhajan Lal Sharma
content: He said technology had an immense potential to transform agriculture sector in the State on a large scale

title: CJI bats for structured national registry of former judges willing to serve in ADR
content: Mediation, Lok Adalats, Arbitration, Conciliation: these are not the back door to justice. For millions of Indians, they are the only door, says Chief Justice of India Surya Kant

title: Unite and resist ring road project in Amaravati, AIKS leader Ashok  Dhawale exhorts farmers
content: Questioning why provisions of the Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition Act-2013 are not being followed, particularly the requirement of Gram Sabha consultations and prior consent of landowners, he demands that farmers be given compensation at four times the market rate

title: NCSC seeks report on Mamata Banerjee's alleged 'derogatory' remark against SC community
content: The NCSC has written to the Chief Secretary of West Bengal and the Director General of Police, directing them to submit a detailed report within three days of the receipt of the notice

title: BJP attacking Bengali culture, pride: Kejriwal; Modi won’t have his way in Bengal: Tejashwi
content: AAP convenor Arvind Kejriwal and RJD leader Tejaswi Yadav are campaigning in the State in support of Trinamool Congress even as Congress leader Rahul Gandhi has launched scathing attacks on Trinamool Congress government and Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: Drug menace emerges as key plank in Amit Shah’s campaign for Punjab polls
content: Top sources in the BJP have told The Hindu that Mr. Shah will be visiting the State as often as once a month, through the year and to each district

title: For two women, a fight for justice in Bengal poll contest
content: Contesting from rival parties, BJP’s Ratna Debnath and CMI(M)’s Sabina Yasmin have turned personal loss into broader questions of safety, governance, and State accountability.

title: Experts seek data-led approach to tackle public health risks
content: The webinar discussed challenges from infectious diseases to NCDs, climate-linked risks, and the growing role of AI and policy in building resilient healthcare systems

title: Union government to resume talks on Ladakh on May 22
content: Amit Shah to visit Ladakh soon; the Ladakh-Centre talks hit a stalemate after violence erupted in Leh in September last year, which left four locals dead and over 80, including security personnel, injured

title: "They Had Child Together, Now She Says Rape?" Supreme Court On Live-In Relationship
content: The observation was made by Justice BV Nagarathna while hearing a petition filed by a woman accusing a man of rape, assault, and exploitation.

title: "They Had Child Together, Now She Says Rape?" Supreme Court On Live-In Relationship
content: The observation was made by Justice BV Nagarathna while hearing a petition filed by a woman accusing a man of rape, assault, and exploitation.

title: "Ready To Bear Consequences": Full Text Of Arvind Kejriwal's Letter To Judge
content: Arvind Kejriwal has written a letter to Justice Swarna Kanta Sharma, expressing doubts over whether he would get justice in her court.

title: "I'll Bury You": Madhya Pradesh Minister's Brother Threatens Woman Officer
content: The minister's brother not only abused and threatened the woman officer, but physically lunged at her and blocked her path.

title: "No Hope Of Justice": Arvind Kejriwal's "Satyagraha" Move Against Judge
content: In a letter to Justice Sharma, Arvind Kejriwal claimed that his hope of receiving justice from her has been shattered.

title: Man, 30, Found Dead With Gunshot Wound Inside Scorpio In Greater Noida
content: The police said they are questioning family members and gathering details about the victim's mental and family condition.

title: This New AI Tool Reduces Dispatcher Workload From 10 Hours To 60 Minutes
content: PILOT uses a system of 11 specialised AI agents to handle key functions such as planning routes, managing drivers, recovering failed deliveries and reconciling invoices.

title: 'Partial, Lacks Credibility': Iranian Lawmaker's Big Allegation Against 'Mediator' Pakistan
content: The Iranian lawmaker highlighted instances where Islamabad failed to criticise the US when it did not fulfil commitments, including on Lebanon and blocked assets.

title: Mamata Banerjee "Fought 82 Cases, Lost All": Amit Shah On Court Threat
content: Amit Shah expressed strong confidence that the BJP will form the government in the state with a "full and decisive majority."

title: Centre Likely To Roll Out Relief Package For Sectors Hit By Iran War
content: Under the package, an airline credit scheme, worth Rs 5,000 crore, is under consideration. Airlines may get access to Centre-backed credit line.

title: Video: "Keep Onion In Your Pocket": Jyotiraditya Scindia's Summer Advice
content: Weather officials said that a heatwave has gripped most parts of the state, with maximum temperatures across many regions currently ranging between 44 degrees Celsius and 46 degrees Celsius, remaining above normal.

title: Iranian Foreign Minister Meets Asim Munir After Landing In Islamabad
content: There was no official word from Pakistan about the meeting which apparently focused on the ongoing peace efforts by Islamabad to arrange the second round of talks between the US and Iran.

title: Woman Ends 28 Years Of Marriage, Calls Husband's Sister His 'Second Wife'
content: The husband has challenged a one-sided divorce decree granted on the basis of what he alleges was misleading evidence presented by his wife.

title: Trump Event Shooting LIVE Updates: Accused Shooter Likely Targeted Trump And His Top Officials
content: Shooting at Trump's event live updates: Donald Trump was rushed out of the White House Correspondents' Association dinner after suspected gunshots were heard at the event.

title: 'Mood In India Similar': Congress MLA's Shocker On Trump Event Shooting
content: The shooter, Cole Tomas Allen, 31, had arrived at the Washington Hilton carrying three weapons, namely two handguns and a shotgun

title: Who Was Sheikh Yousuf Afridi, Lashkar Terrorist Shot Dead By Gunmen In Pakistan
content: The killing adds to a series of similar attacks on high-profile terrorists in Pakistan in recent months.

title: Kejriwal tried to stop defections, but it was a case of too little, too late
content: arvind Kejriwal made last-ditch efforts to salvage the situation by reaching out to most of them . that he saw him as a loyalist who wouldn't switch sides, people aware of the developments said . he said he has submitted a petition to RS chairman seeking disqualification .

title: Nasa satellite spots gleaming halo around New Zealand’s Chatham Islands
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More

title: Isro plans to send civilians with STEM backgrounds to space
content: Chethan Kumar is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Times of India. Aside from specialising in Space & Science, he has reported extensively on varied topics, with special focus on defence, policy and data stories. He has covered multiple elections, too. As a young democracy grows out of adolescence, Chethan feels, there are reels of tales emerging which need to be captured. To do this, he alternates between the mundane goings-on of the Common Man and the wonder-filled worlds of scientists and scamsters, politicians and soldiers. In a career spanning nearly 18 years, he has reported from multiple datelines — Houston, Florida, Kochi, Hyderabad, Chennai, Sriharikota (AP), NH-1 (J&K Highway), New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Raichur, Bhatkal, Mysuru, Chamarajanagar, to name a few —  but is based out of Bengaluru, India’s science capital that also hosts the ISRO HQ. Read More
